titleStmt.title: Letter from Henry H. Billiter to John R. Moorhead, 13 June 1916.
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Henry H. Billiter

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
This letter was written by Henry H. Billiter to John R. Moorhead (1859-1936),crown solicitor for Belfast.
The letter details the exact travel expenses incurred by Billiter as a result of being called as a witness to a trial as a representative of Messers. Cox & Co. The expenses are itemised and come to a total of £6-7-9.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary’s Office Registered papers, CSO-RP-1916-5615-11091-2

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
D Dept
16. Charing Cross.
London.
SW. 13 June 1916 Rev V Wheeler Dear Sir In reply to your letter dated
7th inst my expenses as representative
of Messrs Cox & Co in connection with
above trial are approximately as
follow 1st Class return to Belfast £4-1-6
Cab fare to Euston 4/-
do on return 4/-
Breakfast on train 2·9
Lunch do 3·3
Dinner do 3·3
Hotel expenses 10·7
(including tips)
Dinner on boat on
return journey 3·5
Total £6-7-9.
Trusting to hear from you shortly Yours faithfully Henry H Billiter John R Moorhead Esq
Crown Solicitor
